A traumatic brain injury from a car accident doesn't just affect you physically — it can reshape your entire life, your relationships, your ability to work, and your sense of self. If this has happened to you or someone you love in Chicago, you don't have to face it alone.

Understanding your legal rights after a TBI is critical. Illinois law gives you a window to act, and having the right attorney — one who truly understands severe brain injury cases — can mean the difference between a settlement that barely covers your bills and one that accounts for your long-term care, lost income, and quality of life.

WreckMatch connects car accident victims with experienced attorneys across Illinois who handle exactly these kinds of serious cases. The service is free, fast, and built for people in tough situations.

reddit_title: Got a TBI from a car accident in Chicago — what should I know about the legal process before talking to an attorney?
reddit_body:
TBIs are uniquely complicated in personal injury cases because symptoms can be delayed, imaging doesn't always show the full picture, and insurers often push back hard on the severity. The most important thing you can do right now is get thorough medical documentation — every ER visit, every specialist, every symptom journal entry matters when building your claim.

In Illinois, you generally have two years from the date of the accident to file a personal injury lawsuit, but don't let that timeline give you a false sense of ease. Evidence degrades, witnesses forget details, and getting an attorney involved early means they can help preserve critical records and deal with insurance adjusters so you don't accidentally say something that hurts your case.

If you're unsure where to find an attorney who actually has experience with severe TBI cases specifically, check out wreckmatch.com — it's a free matching service that connects accident victims with vetted attorneys who handle serious injury claims. No obligation, no cost to use.
